methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
DroneShield is a publicly traded defense and security company founded by Brian Hearing and John Franklin. The company has raised over $100 million to date. The comparable universe for valuation purposes includes 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies in the Defense & Security sector, with top private comparables including Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI. However, the peer set presents meaningful constraints for traditional valuation methods.

The peer-median approach is not applicable in this instance. While 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security broadly, filtering for stage alignment, adjacent-stage proximity, or similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category—the minimum required for robust median benchmarking. A reliance on all-sector median multiples would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of defense subsectors and business models.

The narrow public comparable set (2 tickers) and fragmentation across private peers by stage and scale limit the tractability of traditional multiples-based triangulation. A defensible valuation would require supplementary approaches—such as DCF analysis anchored to revenue visibility and gross margin profiles, or transaction comps from recent M&A activity in drone autonomy and counter-UAS segments—to validate any point estimate. The key uncertainties center on revenue growth trajectories, gross margin sustainability, and the degree to which public market pricing of the two peers reflects comparable risk and liquidity profiles.
